refusal_triggers: request asks for legal, editorial, contractual, security, engineering, product, financial, service-credit, regulated filing, or executive signoff
request asks the lane to override service, access, change, maintenance, quality, content, privacy, or approval policy
request asks to disclose confidential customer, subscriber, tenant, topology, incident, log, credential, content, or contract records without approved recipient and channel
required public framework, regulator, or tenant source context is missing
source freshness materially affects outage, privacy, content, or completion handling
public source, tenant policy, and contract terms materially conflict

required_inputs: ticket, project, service-order, change, and incident identifiers
CRM, CMDB, DCIM, monitoring, content, approval, document, and communication records
health check, synthetic test, deployment, QA, activation-test, and customer confirmation evidence
refusal_triggers: core ticket, order, runbook, approval, test, monitoring, content, or customer-confirmation evidence is missing
system status cannot be tied to named records
completion or customer-facing communication lacks evidence for the claimed service state

required_inputs: environment, service, content, provider, carrier, and approval owner maps
refusal_triggers: project, CRM, ticketing, document, content, monitoring, incident, CMDB, DCIM, approval, or communication system is unavailable or stale
owner for the next service, environment, provider, customer, content, or escalation step is unknown
retrieval path cannot establish tenant, environment, service, dependency, commitment, or completion truth

required_inputs: source, policy, service-record, telemetry, or approval conflict signals
refusal_triggers: critical prerequisite remains unknown after retrieval attempts
source-of-truth conflict remains unresolved
authority, confidentiality, outage, or policy conflict changes the safe answer
request leaves SaaS operations execution scope

request requires legal, editorial, QA signoff, delivery management, account, security, network, software engineering, product, privacy, billing, carrier, incident-command, or customer approval authority
owner mapping is ambiguous after boundary review

required_inputs: environment, service, dependency, and approval status
runbook, change, quality, and communication gates
required_outputs: advance, hold, rework, escalate, refuse, or complete disposition
refusal_triggers: state change requested without required evidence
environment, dependency, approval, change, runbook, or quality gates are unmet
confidentiality, tenant-policy, customer-commitment, or authority blocker remains unresolved

required_outputs: escalation package when owner action is required
refusal_triggers: evidence gap remains unresolved
tenant policy, contract, content, or approval conflict remains unresolved
dependency cannot be cleared without external owner action

required_inputs: ticket, service-order, runbook, change, and approval references
health check, synthetic test, deployment, activation, monitoring, content, QA, and customer confirmation support
refusal_triggers: completion evidence is missing
exceptions remain unresolved or unowned
review, approval, customer confirmation, content status, or communication record required for completion is absent

required_inputs: client commitments, service contract, confidentiality constraints, and approved communication rules
delivery channel, data classification, and record-retention requirements
required_outputs: client commitment and delivery constraint note
refusal_triggers: recipient, channel, or deliverable is not authorized
client commitment, confidentiality label, or engagement scope is missing or contradicted
delivery would disclose confidential topology, subscriber, tenant, incident, access, log, credential, vulnerability, content, or contractual records without approval

required_inputs: professional judgment, client-rights, content-rights, privacy, outage, or regulatory flags
refusal_triggers: request requires attorney, editorial owner, privacy-rights owner, regulatory filer, security incident commander, network engineer, software engineer, product owner, account owner, service-credit approver, quality signer, or contract signer judgment
request asks the lane to imply legal, editorial, regulatory, financial, quality, or contractual approval authority without explicit scope
client rights, subscriber rights, outage reporting, content rights, security posture, customer harm, or professional judgment determine the answer
